config.json-schema-plus.title: JSON Schema Plus
config.json-schema-plus.schemas.descript:
- Provides multilingual dynamic schema matching based on `json.schemas`.
- It allows defining different schema architectures for various language environments via `urls`.
- `url` is the default schema to load if no matching schema is found in `urls`.
config.json-schema-plus.schemas.fileMatc:
An array of file patterns to match against when resolving JSON files to schemas.
- `*` and `**` can be used as a wildcard.
- Exclusion patterns can also be defined and start with `!`.
- A file matches when there is at least one matching pattern and the last matching pattern is not an exclusion pattern.

A file pattern to match against when resolving JSON files to schemas.
- `*` and `**` can be used as a wildcard.
- Exclusion patterns can also be defined and start with `!`.
config.json-schema-plus.schemas.url:
A URL or absolute file path to default schema.
- Can be a relative path (starting with `./`) in workspace and workspace folder settings.
- It will be loaded if no matching schema is found in `urls`.
- Here are some examples:

  ```json
  "url": "./schema/scoop-manifest.en-US.json"
  ```
  ```json
  "url": "https://schema.abgox.com/scoop-manifest.en-US.json"
  ```
config.json-schema-plus.schemas.urls:
Schema architectures for different languages.
- It will load the schema that matches the current language environment.
- Falls back to the default schema defined in `url` if no matching schema is found in `urls`.
config.json-schema-plus.schemas.urls.url:
A URL or absolute file path to a schema.
- Can be a relative path (starting with `./`) in workspace and workspace folder settings.
- Here are some examples:

  ```json
  "url": "./schema/scoop-manifest.en-US.json"
  ```
  ```json
  "url": "https://schema.abgox.com/scoop-manifest.en-US.json"
  ```
